description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between the reddish-brown stone and black recessed channels. The sides of the block are solid and unadorned, with a consistent dark coloration, suggesting a dense, solid construction. The top surface appears rough and textured, likely to provide traction or prevent slipping. The block is substantial in size, likely intended as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment such as a sidewalk or plaza. There are no visible markings, engravings, or signs of significant weathering on the surfaces.
description: A large, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its rough, reddish-brown top surface and solid black sides. The striped pattern and robust build suggest use as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striped, grooved top surface and solid black front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the reddish-brown stone more prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the parallel grooves and solid black rear face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep grooves and the stone's rough texture.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or decorative obstacle
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering or erosion; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned as part of a row of similar blocks
